title: Blockchain and Government Records Management
content:
# Blockchain and Government Records Management

Contributors: sharmila bhatia
Date: 
Subjects: 

Blockchain and Government Records Management Sharmila Bhatia Designing Storage Architectures Meeting September 9, 2019   Background ●  FY18 initiative to assess governments use of blockchain technology in records management  –Review & analyze sources –Identify impact in context of records management policy / guidance  ●  Goal was to give NARA staff a better understanding of the technology and its implications 2   Records Management Implications Records on the Blockchain: The term “recorded information” as described in 44 U.S. Code § 3301b includes all traditional forms of records, regardless of physical form or characteristics, including information created, manipulated, communicated, or stored in digital or electronic form. The blockchain hash, block header, and transactional data could be Federal records. 3   Records Management Implications Records Appraisal:  ● Hash and/or metadata could be records ● Records are appraised on content, not the file format ● Blockchain records may be considered a file format
